summary: This is an extremely brief post consisting of a single quote from a commenter named Razib Kahn on the Fog Creek discussion forums. The quote describes how a developer stopped receiving complaint emails after giving up on trying to be original and simply copying Amazon's shopping system design. The post implicitly endorses the idea that users expect familiar interfaces and that convention trumps innovation in UI design.
key_points: Copying established UI conventions (like Amazon's shopping flow) eliminates user confusion
User complaints are often a signal that you've deviated too far from familiar patterns
Sometimes the best design decision is to stop being original and follow what works
money_quotes: So I finally gave up and created a shopping system exactly like amazon.com, and the e-mails stopped at a screeching halt.
key_insight: When users already know how Amazon works, copying that familiar pattern is better UX than inventing something novel.
